scope: Route-identity contract for the next MORI AsyncLL staged dX overlap attempt.

source_inspection.finding: MORI get_dispatch_src_token_pos is a flat source token index using FlatTokenIndex(pe, localTokId) with stride MaxNumTokensToSend(). MaxNumTokensToSend() is worldSize * maxNumInpTokenPerRank, so the metadata identifies the source input row, not the selected top-k route slot inside a token.
source_inspection.implication: For normal [tokens, topk=6] routing, one source token can have multiple expert slots landing on the same destination rank. Source-position alignment can therefore be ambiguous, and (src_pos, full top-k ids) can still duplicate rows.

prior_evidence.read: Independent AsyncLL handles conserve route multisets in the standalone probe, but normal topk=6 rows do not provide a unique per-route identity. Integrated learned-router S4096 then fails both route_key and src_pos alignment gates.

changes: Added --route-flatten to dispatch logical [tokens, topk] routes as [tokens * topk, 1].
Added route-flat payload fields for source row, source rank, logical token, and route slot.
Switched source-position decoding to MORI op.decode_send_flat_idx() instead of the older hand decode.
Added duplicate src_pos and duplicate route-key counts to the probe summary.

remote_gate.purpose: Check whether flattened topk=1 routing removes duplicate source-position rows and lets independent x/grad/topk AsyncLL handles align by source row at S4096.
expected_reject_signal: Any duplicate_src_pos rows remain in route_flatten mode
Independent handles do not align after source-position sort
Route-key multisets differ across x/grad/topk handles
remote_gate.next_action_if_passes: Prototype a flattened topk=1 backward dX-return path, then rerun seeded S4096 sync-vs-AsyncLL parity before any throughput A/B.
remote_gate.next_action_if_fails: Reject independent-handle AsyncLL overlap until MORI exposes shared route/order metadata or a per-dispatched-row route id from the production dispatch handle.

remote_result.read: Flattened topk=1 routing gives independent AsyncLL handles a unique per-route source row and aligns after source-position sorting on the 4x S4096 probe. This does not prove the training bridge yet; it promotes flattened-route dX return to the next seeded sync-vs-AsyncLL parity branch.
